description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly gray surface, featuring patches of green moss or lichen on its upper areas. The rock has a somewhat rounded top and angular sides, with visible cracks and subtle surface texturing that suggest natural weathering. The coloration transitions smoothly from gray to green, giving it a slightly organic appearance. The surface is matte and rough, with no visible polish or artificial treatment. The rock appears solid and heavy, likely composed of dense stone material. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A gray, irregular rock with green mossy patches and visible cracks, featuring a rough, matte surface and natural weathering.
description_view_0: The rock shows a prominent flat gray surface with green moss on the upper left and visible cracks on the lower right.
description_view_1: This side highlights a more rounded, moss-covered area with less visible cracking and a softer transition between gray and green.
description_view_2: The view presents a mostly smooth, gray surface with a small patch of green at the top, and minimal visible cracks.
description_view_3: This angle reveals a large green mossy area on the upper left, a broad gray surface, and a few fine cracks on the lower right.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
